dbPassword.description: A temporary password that authorizes the user name returned by DbUser to log on to the database DbName.

dbUser.description: A database user name that is authorized to log on to the database DbName using the password DbPassword. If the specified DbUser exists in the database, the new user name has the same database privileges as the the user named in DbUser. By default, the user is added to PUBLIC.

expiration.description: The date and time the password in DbPassword expires.

nextRefreshTime.description: The date and time of when the DbUser and DbPassword authorization refreshes.

accountsWithProvisionedRestoreAccess.description: All of the Amazon Web Services accounts that have access to restore a snapshot to a provisioned cluster.

nextToken.description: If your initial ListTableRestoreStatus operation returns a nextToken, you can include the returned nextToken in following ListTableRestoreStatus operations. This will returns results on the next page.

message.description: A message that explains the returned status. For example, if the status of the operation is FAILED, the message explains why the operation failed.

namespaceName.description: The namespace of the table being restored from.

newTableName.description: The name of the table to create from the restore operation.

progressInMegaBytes.description: The amount of data restored to the new table so far, in megabytes (MB).

recoveryPointId.description: The ID of the recovery point being restored from.

requestTime.description: The time that the table restore request was made, in Universal Coordinated Time (UTC).

snapshotName.description: The name of the snapshot being restored from.

sourceDatabaseName.description: The name of the source database being restored from.

sourceSchemaName.description: The name of the source schema being restored from.

sourceTableName.description: The name of the source table being restored from.

status.description: A value that describes the current state of the table restore request. Possible values are SUCCEEDED, FAILED, CANCELED, PENDING, and IN_PROGRESS.

targetDatabaseName.description: The name of the database to restore to.

targetSchemaName.description: The name of the schema to restore to.

totalDataInMegaBytes.description: The total amount of data to restore to the new table, in megabytes (MB).

workgroupName.description: The name of the workgroup being restored from.

trackName.description: The name of the track. Valid values are current and trailing.

updateTargets.description: An array of UpdateTarget objects to update with the track.

workgroupVersion.description: The workgroup version number for the workgroup release.

usageLimits.description: An array of returned usage limit objects.

amount.description: The limit amount. If time-based, this amount is in RPUs consumed per hour. If data-based, this amount is in terabytes (TB). The value must be a positive number.

breachAction.description: The action that Amazon Redshift Serverless takes when the limit is reached.

period.description: The time period that the amount applies to. A weekly period begins on Sunday. The default is monthly.

resourceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that identifies the Amazon Redshift Serverless resource.

usageLimitArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the resource associated with the usage limit.

usageType.description: The Amazon Redshift Serverless feature to limit.

configParameters.description: An array of parameters to set for advanced control over a database. The options are auto_mv, datestyle, enable_case_sensitive_identifier, enable_user_activity_logging, query_group, search_path, require_ssl, use_fips_ssl, and query monitoring metrics that let you define performance boundaries. For more information about query monitoring rules and available metrics, see Query monitoring metrics for Amazon Redshift Serverless.

crossAccountVpcs.description: A list of VPCs. Each entry is the unique identifier of a virtual private cloud with access to Amazon Redshift Serverless. If all of the VPCs for the grantee are allowed, it shows an asterisk.

port.description: The port that Amazon Redshift Serverless listens on.

enhancedVpcRouting.description: The value that specifies whether to enable enhanced virtual private cloud (VPC) routing, which forces Amazon Redshift Serverless to route traffic through your VPC.

ipAddressType.description: The IP address type that the workgroup supports. Possible values are ipv4 and dualstack.

maxCapacity.description: The maximum data-warehouse capacity Amazon Redshift Serverless uses to serve queries. The max capacity is specified in RPUs.

namespaceName.description: The namespace the workgroup is associated with.

patchVersion.description: The patch version of your Amazon Redshift Serverless workgroup. For more information about patch versions, see Cluster versions for Amazon Redshift.

pendingTrackName.description: The name for the track that you want to assign to the workgroup. When the track changes, the workgroup is switched to the latest workgroup release available for the track. At this point, the track name is applied.

port.description: The custom port to use when connecting to a workgroup. Valid port ranges are 5431-5455 and 8191-8215. The default is 5439.

pricePerformanceTarget.description: An object that represents the price performance target settings for the workgroup.

level.description: The target price performance level for the workgroup. Valid values include 1, 25, 50, 75, and 100. These correspond to the price performance levels LOW_COST, ECONOMICAL, BALANCED, RESOURCEFUL, and HIGH_PERFORMANCE.

status.description: Whether the price performance target is enabled for the workgroup.

publiclyAccessible.description: A value that specifies whether the workgroup can be accessible from a public network.

scheduledAction.description: The returned ScheduledAction object that describes the properties of a scheduled action.

namespaceName.description: The end time in UTC when the schedule is no longer active. After this time, the scheduled action does not trigger.

nextInvocations.description: An array of timestamps of when the next scheduled actions will trigger.

roleArn.description: The ARN of the IAM role to assume to run the scheduled action. This IAM role must have permission to run the Amazon Redshift Serverless API operation in the scheduled action. This IAM role must allow the Amazon Redshift scheduler to schedule creating snapshots. (Principal scheduler.redshift.amazonaws.com) to assume permissions on your behalf. For more information about the IAM role to use with the Amazon Redshift scheduler, see Using Identity-Based Policies for Amazon Redshift in the Amazon Redshift Management Guide

schedule.description:
The schedule for a one-time (at timestamp format) or recurring (cron format) scheduled action. Schedule invocations must be separated by at least one hour. Times are in UTC.

  • Format of at timestamp is yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss. For example, 2016-03-04T17:27:00.

  • Format of cron expression is (Minutes Hours Day-of-month Month Day-of-week Year). For example, "(0 10 ? * MON *)". For more information, see Cron Expressions in the Amazon CloudWatch Events User Guide.

at.description: The timestamp of when Amazon Redshift Serverless should run the scheduled action. Timestamp is in UTC. Format of at expression is yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss. For example, 2016-03-04T17:27:00.

cron.description:
The cron expression to use to schedule a recurring scheduled action. Schedule invocations must be separated by at least one hour. Times are in UTC.

Format of cron expressions is (Minutes Hours Day-of-month Month Day-of-week Year). For example, "(0 10 ? * MON *)". For more information, see Cron Expressions in the Amazon CloudWatch Events User Guide.

startTime.description: The start time in UTC when the schedule is active. Before this time, the scheduled action does not trigger.

targetAction.description:
A JSON format string of the Amazon Redshift Serverless API operation with input parameters. The following is an example of a target action.

"{"CreateSnapshot": {"NamespaceName": "sampleNamespace","SnapshotName": "sampleSnapshot", "retentionPeriod": "1"}}"

createSnapshot.description: The parameters that you can use to configure a scheduled action to create a snapshot. For more information about creating a scheduled action, see CreateScheduledAction.

namespaceName.description: The name of the namespace for which you want to configure a scheduled action to create a snapshot.

retentionPeriod.description: The retention period of the snapshot created by the scheduled action.

snapshotNamePrefix.description: A string prefix that is attached to the name of the snapshot created by the scheduled action. The final name of the snapshot is the string prefix appended by the date and time of when the snapshot was created.

tags.description: An array of Tag objects to associate with the snapshot.

workgroupName.description: The name of the workgroup.

workgroupVersion.description: The Amazon Redshift Serverless version of your workgroup. For more information about Amazon Redshift Serverless versions, seeCluster versions for Amazon Redshift.

endpointArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the VPC endpoint.

endpointCreateTime.description: The time that the endpoint was created.

port.description: The port number on which Amazon Redshift Serverless accepts incoming connections.

subnetIds.description: The unique identifier of subnets where Amazon Redshift Serverless choose to deploy the VPC endpoint.

vpcEndpoint.description: The connection endpoint for connecting to Amazon Redshift Serverless.

networkInterfaces.description: One or more network interfaces of the endpoint. Also known as an interface endpoint.

vpcEndpointId.description: The connection endpoint ID for connecting to Amazon Redshift Serverless.

vpcId.description: The VPC identifier that the endpoint is associated with.

vpcSecurityGroups.description: The security groups associated with the endpoint.

workgroupName.description: The name of the workgroup associated with the endpoint.

adminPasswordSecretArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the namespace's admin user credentials secret.

adminPasswordSecretKmsKeyId.description: The ID of the Key Management Service (KMS) key used to encrypt and store the namespace's admin credentials secret.

adminUsername.description: The username of the administrator for the first database created in the namespace.

catalogArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Glue Data Catalog associated with the namespace enabled with Amazon Redshift federated permissions.

creationDate.description: The date of when the namespace was created.

dbName.description: The name of the first database created in the namespace.

defaultIamRoleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role to set as a default in the namespace.

iamRoles.description: A list of IAM roles to associate with the namespace.

kmsKeyId.description: The ID of the Amazon Web Services Key Management Service key used to encrypt your data.

lakehouseRegistrationStatus.description: The status of the lakehouse registration for the namespace. Indicates whether the namespace is successfully registered with Amazon Redshift federated permissions.

logExports.description: The types of logs the namespace can export. Available export types are User log, Connection log, and User activity log.

namespaceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) associated with a namespace.

namespaceName.description: The name of the namespace. Must be between 3-64 alphanumeric characters in lowercase, and it cannot be a reserved word. A list of reserved words can be found in Reserved Words in the Amazon Redshift Database Developer Guide.
The end time in UTC when the schedule is no longer active. After this time, the scheduled action does not trigger.

nextInvocations.description: An array of timestamps of when the next scheduled actions will trigger.

roleArn.description: The ARN of the IAM role to assume to run the scheduled action. This IAM role must have permission to run the Amazon Redshift Serverless API operation in the scheduled action. This IAM role must allow the Amazon Redshift scheduler to schedule creating snapshots. (Principal scheduler.redshift.amazonaws.com) to assume permissions on your behalf. For more information about the IAM role to use with the Amazon Redshift scheduler, see Using Identity-Based Policies for Amazon Redshift in the Amazon Redshift Management Guide

schedule.description:
The schedule for a one-time (at timestamp format) or recurring (cron format) scheduled action. Schedule invocations must be separated by at least one hour. Times are in UTC.

  • Format of at timestamp is yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss. For example, 2016-03-04T17:27:00.

  • Format of cron expression is (Minutes Hours Day-of-month Month Day-of-week Year). For example, "(0 10 ? * MON *)". For more information, see Cron Expressions in the Amazon CloudWatch Events User Guide.

at.description: The timestamp of when Amazon Redshift Serverless should run the scheduled action. Timestamp is in UTC. Format of at expression is yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss. For example, 2016-03-04T17:27:00.

cron.description:
The cron expression to use to schedule a recurring scheduled action. Schedule invocations must be separated by at least one hour. Times are in UTC.

Format of cron expressions is (Minutes Hours Day-of-month Month Day-of-week Year). For example, "(0 10 ? * MON *)". For more information, see Cron Expressions in the Amazon CloudWatch Events User Guide.

startTime.description: The start time in UTC when the schedule is active. Before this time, the scheduled action does not trigger.

targetAction.description:
A JSON format string of the Amazon Redshift Serverless API operation with input parameters. The following is an example of a target action.

"{"CreateSnapshot": {"NamespaceName": "sampleNamespace","SnapshotName": "sampleSnapshot", "retentionPeriod": "1"}}"

createSnapshot.description: The parameters that you can use to configure a scheduled action to create a snapshot. For more information about creating a scheduled action, see CreateScheduledAction.

namespaceName.description: The name of the namespace for which you want to configure a scheduled action to create a snapshot.

retentionPeriod.description: The retention period of the snapshot created by the scheduled action.

snapshotNamePrefix.description: A string prefix that is attached to the name of the snapshot created by the scheduled action. The final name of the snapshot is the string prefix appended by the date and time of when the snapshot was created.

tags.description: An array of Tag objects to associate with the snapshot.

accountsWithProvisionedRestoreAccess.description: All of the Amazon Web Services accounts that have access to restore a snapshot to a provisioned cluster.

accountsWithRestoreAccess.description: All of the Amazon Web Services accounts that have access to restore a snapshot to a namespace.

actualIncrementalBackupSizeInMegaBytes.description: The size of the incremental backup in megabytes.

adminPasswordSecretArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the namespace's admin user credentials secret.

adminPasswordSecretKmsKeyId.description: The ID of the Key Management Service (KMS) key used to encrypt and store the namespace's admin credentials secret.

adminUsername.description: The username of the database within a snapshot.

backupProgressInMegaBytes.description: The size in megabytes of the data that has been backed up to a snapshot.

currentBackupRateInMegaBytesPerSecond.description: The rate at which data is backed up into a snapshot in megabytes per second.

elapsedTimeInSeconds.description: The amount of time it took to back up data into a snapshot.

estimatedSecondsToCompletion.description: The estimated amount of seconds until the snapshot completes backup.

kmsKeyId.description: The unique identifier of the KMS key used to encrypt the snapshot.

namespaceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the namespace the snapshot was created from.

namespaceName.description: The name of the namepsace.

ownerAccount.description: The owner Amazon Web Services; account of the snapshot.

snapshotArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the snapshot.

snapshotCreateTime.description: The timestamp of when the snapshot was created.

snapshotRemainingDays.description: The amount of days until the snapshot is deleted.

snapshotRetentionPeriod.description: The period of time, in days, of how long the snapshot is retained.

snapshotRetentionStartTime.description: The timestamp of when data within the snapshot started getting retained.

totalBackupSizeInMegaBytes.description: The total size, in megabytes, of how big the snapshot is.

destinationKmsKeyId.description: The ID of the KMS key to use to encrypt your snapshots in the destination Amazon Web Services Region.

destinationRegion.description: The destination Amazon Web Services Region to copy snapshots to.

namespaceName.description: The name of the namespace to copy snapshots from in the source Amazon Web Services Region.

snapshotCopyConfigurationArn.description: The ARN of the snapshot copy configuration object.

snapshotCopyConfigurationId.description: The ID of the snapshot copy configuration object.

snapshotRetentionPeriod.description: The retention period of snapshots that are copied to the destination Amazon Web Services Region.

amount.description: The limit amount. If time-based, this amount is in RPUs consumed per hour. If data-based, this amount is in terabytes (TB). The value must be a positive number.

breachAction.description: The action that Amazon Redshift Serverless takes when the limit is reached.

period.description: The time period that the amount applies to. A weekly period begins on Sunday. The default is monthly.

resourceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that identifies the Amazon Redshift Serverless resource.

usageLimitArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the resource associated with the usage limit.

usageType.description: The Amazon Redshift Serverless feature to limit.

baseCapacity.description: The base data warehouse capacity of the workgroup in Redshift Processing Units (RPUs).

configParameters.description: An array of parameters to set for advanced control over a database. The options are auto_mv, datestyle, enable_case_sensitive_identifier, enable_user_activity_logging, query_group, search_path, require_ssl, use_fips_ssl, and query monitoring metrics that let you define performance boundaries. For more information about query monitoring rules and available metrics, see Query monitoring metrics for Amazon Redshift Serverless.

crossAccountVpcs.description: A list of VPCs. Each entry is the unique identifier of a virtual private cloud with access to Amazon Redshift Serverless. If all of the VPCs for the grantee are allowed, it shows an asterisk.

customDomainCertificateArn.description: The custom domain name’s certificate Amazon resource name (ARN).

customDomainName.description: The custom domain name associated with the workgroup.

endpoint.description: The endpoint that is created from the workgroup.

address.description: The DNS address of the VPC endpoint.

port.description: The port that Amazon Redshift Serverless listens on.

enhancedVpcRouting.description: The value that specifies whether to enable enhanced virtual private cloud (VPC) routing, which forces Amazon Redshift Serverless to route traffic through your VPC.

ipAddressType.description: The IP address type that the workgroup supports. Possible values are ipv4 and dualstack.

maxCapacity.description: The maximum data-warehouse capacity Amazon Redshift Serverless uses to serve queries. The max capacity is specified in RPUs.

namespaceName.description: The namespace the workgroup is associated with.

patchVersion.description: The patch version of your Amazon Redshift Serverless workgroup. For more information about patch versions, see Cluster versions for Amazon Redshift.

pendingTrackName.description: The name for the track that you want to assign to the workgroup. When the track changes, the workgroup is switched to the latest workgroup release available for the track. At this point, the track name is applied.

port.description: The custom port to use when connecting to a workgroup. Valid port ranges are 5431-5455 and 8191-8215. The default is 5439.

pricePerformanceTarget.description: An object that represents the price performance target settings for the workgroup.

level.description: The target price performance level for the workgroup. Valid values include 1, 25, 50, 75, and 100. These correspond to the price performance levels LOW_COST, ECONOMICAL, BALANCED, RESOURCEFUL, and HIGH_PERFORMANCE.

status.description: Whether the price performance target is enabled for the workgroup.

publiclyAccessible.description: A value that specifies whether the workgroup can be accessible from a public network.

securityGroupIds.description: An array of security group IDs to associate with the workgroup.

subnetIds.description: An array of subnet IDs the workgroup is associated with.

trackName.description: The name of the track for the workgroup.

workgroupArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that links to the workgroup.

workgroupName.description: The name of the workgroup.

workgroupVersion.description: The Amazon Redshift Serverless version of your workgroup. For more information about Amazon Redshift Serverless versions, seeCluster versions for Amazon Redshift.

creationDate.description: The date of when the namespace was created.

dbName.description: The name of the first database created in the namespace.

defaultIamRoleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role to set as a default in the namespace.

iamRoles.description: A list of IAM roles to associate with the namespace.

kmsKeyId.description: The ID of the Amazon Web Services Key Management Service key used to encrypt your data.

lakehouseRegistrationStatus.description: The status of the lakehouse registration for the namespace. Indicates whether the namespace is successfully registered with Amazon Redshift federated permissions.

logExports.description: The types of logs the namespace can export. Available export types are User log, Connection log, and User activity log.

namespaceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) associated with a namespace.

namespaceName.description: The name of the namespace. Must be between 3-64 alphanumeric characters in lowercase, and it cannot be a reserved word. A list of reserved words can be found in Reserved Words in the Amazon Redshift Database Developer Guide.

namespaceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the namespace the recovery point is associated with.

namespaceName.description: The name of the namespace the recovery point is associated with.

recoveryPointCreateTime.description: The time the recovery point is created.

recoveryPointId.description: The unique identifier of the recovery point.

totalSizeInMegaBytes.description: The total size of the data in the recovery point in megabytes.

workgroupName.description: The name of the workgroup the recovery point is associated with.

capacity.description: The number of Redshift Processing Units (RPUs) to reserve.

endDate.description: The end date for the serverless reservation. This date is one year after the start date that you specify.

offering.description: The type of offering for the reservation. The offering class determines the payment schedule for the reservation.

duration.description: The duration, in seconds, for which the reservation reserves the RPUs.

hourlyCharge.description: The rate you are charged for each hour the reservation is active.

offeringType.description: Determines the payment schedule for the reservation.

upfrontCharge.description: The up-front price you are charged for the reservation.

reservationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that uniquely identifies the serverless reservation.

reservationId.description: The identifier that uniquely identifies the serverless reservation.

startDate.description: The start date for the serverless reservation. This is the date you created the reservation.

status.description:
The status of the reservation. Possible values include the following:

  • payment-pending

  • active

  • payment-failed

  • retired

reservationOffering.description: The returned reservation offering. The offering determines the payment schedule for the reservation.

duration.description: The duration, in seconds, for which the reservation reserves the RPUs.

hourlyCharge.description: The rate you are charged for each hour the reservation is active.

offeringType.description: Determines the payment schedule for the reservation.

upfrontCharge.description: The up-front price you are charged for the reservation.

resourceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the policy.

namespaceName.description: The end time in UTC when the schedule is no longer active. After this time, the scheduled action does not trigger.

nextInvocations.description: An array of timestamps of when the next scheduled actions will trigger.

roleArn.description: The ARN of the IAM role to assume to run the scheduled action. This IAM role must have permission to run the Amazon Redshift Serverless API operation in the scheduled action. This IAM role must allow the Amazon Redshift scheduler to schedule creating snapshots. (Principal scheduler.redshift.amazonaws.com) to assume permissions on your behalf. For more information about the IAM role to use with the Amazon Redshift scheduler, see Using Identity-Based Policies for Amazon Redshift in the Amazon Redshift Management Guide

schedule.description:
The schedule for a one-time (at timestamp format) or recurring (cron format) scheduled action. Schedule invocations must be separated by at least one hour. Times are in UTC.

  • Format of at timestamp is yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss. For example, 2016-03-04T17:27:00.

  • Format of cron expression is (Minutes Hours Day-of-month Month Day-of-week Year). For example, "(0 10 ? * MON *)". For more information, see Cron Expressions in the Amazon CloudWatch Events User Guide.

at.description: The timestamp of when Amazon Redshift Serverless should run the scheduled action. Timestamp is in UTC. Format of at expression is yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss. For example, 2016-03-04T17:27:00.

cron.description:
The cron expression to use to schedule a recurring scheduled action. Schedule invocations must be separated by at least one hour. Times are in UTC.

Format of cron expressions is (Minutes Hours Day-of-month Month Day-of-week Year). For example, "(0 10 ? * MON *)". For more information, see Cron Expressions in the Amazon CloudWatch Events User Guide.

startTime.description: The start time in UTC when the schedule is active. Before this time, the scheduled action does not trigger.

targetAction.description:
A JSON format string of the Amazon Redshift Serverless API operation with input parameters. The following is an example of a target action.

"{"CreateSnapshot": {"NamespaceName": "sampleNamespace","SnapshotName": "sampleSnapshot", "retentionPeriod": "1"}}"

createSnapshot.description: The parameters that you can use to configure a scheduled action to create a snapshot. For more information about creating a scheduled action, see CreateScheduledAction.

namespaceName.description: The name of the namespace for which you want to configure a scheduled action to create a snapshot.

retentionPeriod.description: The retention period of the snapshot created by the scheduled action.

snapshotNamePrefix.description: A string prefix that is attached to the name of the snapshot created by the scheduled action. The final name of the snapshot is the string prefix appended by the date and time of when the snapshot was created.

tags.description: An array of Tag objects to associate with the snapshot.

tableRestoreStatus.description: The returned TableRestoreStatus object that contains information about the status of your RestoreTableFromSnapshot request.

message.description: A message that explains the returned status. For example, if the status of the operation is FAILED, the message explains why the operation failed.

namespaceName.description: The namespace of the table being restored from.

newTableName.description: The name of the table to create from the restore operation.

progressInMegaBytes.description: The amount of data restored to the new table so far, in megabytes (MB).

recoveryPointId.description: The ID of the recovery point being restored from.

requestTime.description: The time that the table restore request was made, in Universal Coordinated Time (UTC).

snapshotName.description: The name of the snapshot being restored from.

sourceDatabaseName.description: The name of the source database being restored from.

sourceSchemaName.description: The name of the source schema being restored from.

sourceTableName.description: The name of the source table being restored from.

status.description: A value that describes the current state of the table restore request. Possible values are SUCCEEDED, FAILED, CANCELED, PENDING, and IN_PROGRESS.

targetDatabaseName.description: The name of the database to restore to.

targetSchemaName.description: The name of the schema to restore to.

totalDataInMegaBytes.description: The total amount of data to restore to the new table, in megabytes (MB).

workgroupName.description: The name of the workgroup being restored from.

trackName.description: The name of the track. Valid values are current and trailing.

updateTargets.description: An array of UpdateTarget objects to update with the track.

workgroupVersion.description: The workgroup version number for the workgroup release.

amount.description: The limit amount. If time-based, this amount is in RPUs consumed per hour. If data-based, this amount is in terabytes (TB). The value must be a positive number.

breachAction.description: The action that Amazon Redshift Serverless takes when the limit is reached.

period.description: The time period that the amount applies to. A weekly period begins on Sunday. The default is monthly.

catalogArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Glue Data Catalog associated with the lakehouse configuration.

lakehouseIdcApplicationArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM Identity Center application used for enabling Amazon Web Services IAM Identity Center trusted identity propagation.

lakehouseRegistrationStatus.description: The current status of the lakehouse registration. Indicates whether the namespace is successfully registered with Amazon Redshift federated permissions.

namespaceName.description: The name of the namespace.

adminPasswordSecretArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the namespace's admin user credentials secret.

adminPasswordSecretKmsKeyId.description: The ID of the Key Management Service (KMS) key used to encrypt and store the namespace's admin credentials secret.

adminUsername.description: The username of the administrator for the first database created in the namespace.

catalogArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the Glue Data Catalog associated with the namespace enabled with Amazon Redshift federated permissions.

creationDate.description: The date of when the namespace was created.

dbName.description: The name of the first database created in the namespace.

defaultIamRoleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the IAM role to set as a default in the namespace.

iamRoles.description: A list of IAM roles to associate with the namespace.

kmsKeyId.description: The ID of the Amazon Web Services Key Management Service key used to encrypt your data.

lakehouseRegistrationStatus.description: The status of the lakehouse registration for the namespace. Indicates whether the namespace is successfully registered with Amazon Redshift federated permissions.

logExports.description: The types of logs the namespace can export. Available export types are User log, Connection log, and User activity log.

namespaceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) associated with a namespace.

namespaceName.description: The name of the namespace. Must be between 3-64 alphanumeric characters in lowercase, and it cannot be a reserved word. A list of reserved words can be found in Reserved Words in the Amazon Redshift Database Developer Guide.
The end time in UTC when the schedule is no longer active. After this time, the scheduled action does not trigger.

nextInvocations.description: An array of timestamps of when the next scheduled actions will trigger.

roleArn.description: The ARN of the IAM role to assume to run the scheduled action. This IAM role must have permission to run the Amazon Redshift Serverless API operation in the scheduled action. This IAM role must allow the Amazon Redshift scheduler to schedule creating snapshots. (Principal scheduler.redshift.amazonaws.com) to assume permissions on your behalf. For more information about the IAM role to use with the Amazon Redshift scheduler, see Using Identity-Based Policies for Amazon Redshift in the Amazon Redshift Management Guide

schedule.description:
The schedule for a one-time (at timestamp format) or recurring (cron format) scheduled action. Schedule invocations must be separated by at least one hour. Times are in UTC.

  • Format of at timestamp is yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss. For example, 2016-03-04T17:27:00.

  • Format of cron expression is (Minutes Hours Day-of-month Month Day-of-week Year). For example, "(0 10 ? * MON *)". For more information, see Cron Expressions in the Amazon CloudWatch Events User Guide.

at.description: The timestamp of when Amazon Redshift Serverless should run the scheduled action. Timestamp is in UTC. Format of at expression is yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss. For example, 2016-03-04T17:27:00.

cron.description:
The cron expression to use to schedule a recurring scheduled action. Schedule invocations must be separated by at least one hour. Times are in UTC.

Format of cron expressions is (Minutes Hours Day-of-month Month Day-of-week Year). For example, "(0 10 ? * MON *)". For more information, see Cron Expressions in the Amazon CloudWatch Events User Guide.

startTime.description: The start time in UTC when the schedule is active. Before this time, the scheduled action does not trigger.

targetAction.description:
A JSON format string of the Amazon Redshift Serverless API operation with input parameters. The following is an example of a target action.

"{"CreateSnapshot": {"NamespaceName": "sampleNamespace","SnapshotName": "sampleSnapshot", "retentionPeriod": "1"}}"

createSnapshot.description: The parameters that you can use to configure a scheduled action to create a snapshot. For more information about creating a scheduled action, see CreateScheduledAction.

namespaceName.description: The name of the namespace for which you want to configure a scheduled action to create a snapshot.

retentionPeriod.description: The retention period of the snapshot created by the scheduled action.

snapshotNamePrefix.description: A string prefix that is attached to the name of the snapshot created by the scheduled action. The final name of the snapshot is the string prefix appended by the date and time of when the snapshot was created.

tags.description: An array of Tag objects to associate with the snapshot.

accountsWithProvisionedRestoreAccess.description: All of the Amazon Web Services accounts that have access to restore a snapshot to a provisioned cluster.

accountsWithRestoreAccess.description: All of the Amazon Web Services accounts that have access to restore a snapshot to a namespace.

actualIncrementalBackupSizeInMegaBytes.description: The size of the incremental backup in megabytes.

adminPasswordSecretArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) for the namespace's admin user credentials secret.

adminPasswordSecretKmsKeyId.description: The ID of the Key Management Service (KMS) key used to encrypt and store the namespace's admin credentials secret.

adminUsername.description: The username of the database within a snapshot.

backupProgressInMegaBytes.description: The size in megabytes of the data that has been backed up to a snapshot.

currentBackupRateInMegaBytesPerSecond.description: The rate at which data is backed up into a snapshot in megabytes per second.

elapsedTimeInSeconds.description: The amount of time it took to back up data into a snapshot.

estimatedSecondsToCompletion.description: The estimated amount of seconds until the snapshot completes backup.

kmsKeyId.description: The unique identifier of the KMS key used to encrypt the snapshot.

namespaceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the namespace the snapshot was created from.

namespaceName.description: The name of the namepsace.

ownerAccount.description: The owner Amazon Web Services; account of the snapshot.

snapshotArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the snapshot.

snapshotCreateTime.description: The timestamp of when the snapshot was created.

snapshotRemainingDays.description: The amount of days until the snapshot is deleted.

snapshotRetentionPeriod.description: The period of time, in days, of how long the snapshot is retained.

snapshotRetentionStartTime.description: The timestamp of when data within the snapshot started getting retained.

totalBackupSizeInMegaBytes.description: The total size, in megabytes, of how big the snapshot is.

destinationKmsKeyId.description: The ID of the KMS key to use to encrypt your snapshots in the destination Amazon Web Services Region.

destinationRegion.description: The destination Amazon Web Services Region to copy snapshots to.

namespaceName.description: The name of the namespace to copy snapshots from in the source Amazon Web Services Region.

snapshotCopyConfigurationArn.description: The ARN of the snapshot copy configuration object.

snapshotCopyConfigurationId.description: The ID of the snapshot copy configuration object.

snapshotRetentionPeriod.description: The retention period of snapshots that are copied to the destination Amazon Web Services Region.

amount.description: The limit amount. If time-based, this amount is in RPUs consumed per hour. If data-based, this amount is in terabytes (TB). The value must be a positive number.

breachAction.description: The action that Amazon Redshift Serverless takes when the limit is reached.

period.description: The time period that the amount applies to. A weekly period begins on Sunday. The default is monthly.

resourceArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that identifies the Amazon Redshift Serverless resource.

usageLimitArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the resource associated with the usage limit.

usageType.description: The Amazon Redshift Serverless feature to limit.

baseCapacity.description: The base data warehouse capacity of the workgroup in Redshift Processing Units (RPUs).

configParameters.description: An array of parameters to set for advanced control over a database. The options are auto_mv, datestyle, enable_case_sensitive_identifier, enable_user_activity_logging, query_group, search_path, require_ssl, use_fips_ssl, and query monitoring metrics that let you define performance boundaries. For more information about query monitoring rules and available metrics, see Query monitoring metrics for Amazon Redshift Serverless.

crossAccountVpcs.description: A list of VPCs. Each entry is the unique identifier of a virtual private cloud with access to Amazon Redshift Serverless. If all of the VPCs for the grantee are allowed, it shows an asterisk.

customDomainCertificateArn.description: The custom domain name’s certificate Amazon resource name (ARN).

customDomainName.description: The custom domain name associated with the workgroup.

endpoint.description: The endpoint that is created from the workgroup.

address.description: The DNS address of the VPC endpoint.

port.description: The port that Amazon Redshift Serverless listens on.

enhancedVpcRouting.description: The value that specifies whether to enable enhanced virtual private cloud (VPC) routing, which forces Amazon Redshift Serverless to route traffic through your VPC.

ipAddressType.description: The IP address type that the workgroup supports. Possible values are ipv4 and dualstack.

maxCapacity.description: The maximum data-warehouse capacity Amazon Redshift Serverless uses to serve queries. The max capacity is specified in RPUs.

namespaceName.description: The namespace the workgroup is associated with.

patchVersion.description: The patch version of your Amazon Redshift Serverless workgroup. For more information about patch versions, see Cluster versions for Amazon Redshift.

pendingTrackName.description: The name for the track that you want to assign to the workgroup. When the track changes, the workgroup is switched to the latest workgroup release available for the track. At this point, the track name is applied.

port.description: The custom port to use when connecting to a workgroup. Valid port ranges are 5431-5455 and 8191-8215. The default is 5439.

pricePerformanceTarget.description: An object that represents the price performance target settings for the workgroup.

level.description: The target price performance level for the workgroup. Valid values include 1, 25, 50, 75, and 100. These correspond to the price performance levels LOW_COST, ECONOMICAL, BALANCED, RESOURCEFUL, and HIGH_PERFORMANCE.

status.description: Whether the price performance target is enabled for the workgroup.

publiclyAccessible.description: A value that specifies whether the workgroup can be accessible from a public network.

securityGroupIds.description: An array of security group IDs to associate with the workgroup.

subnetIds.description: An array of subnet IDs the workgroup is associated with.

trackName.description: The name of the track for the workgroup.

workgroupArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) that links to the workgroup.

workgroupName.description: The name of the workgroup.

workgroupVersion.description: The Amazon Redshift Serverless version of your workgroup. For more information about Amazon Redshift Serverless versions, seeCluster versions for Amazon Redshift.
